flags.excerpt: you grant us a non-exclusive, transferable, sub-licensable, royalty-free, and worldwide license
flags.explanation: Meta can use, share, and sublicense anything you post — forever, royalty-free — which may conflict with your right to erasure.

flags.excerpt: We can change these terms, and if we do, we'll let you know
flags.explanation: Continued use of the platform after a change means you automatically accept the new terms, even if you didn't read them.

flags.excerpt: We can remove or restrict access to your content, services, or information
flags.explanation: Meta can suspend your account and restrict your content at their discretion, with limited recourse available to you.

note: Pre-built analysis — based on Meta Terms of Service (2024). Re-analyse for latest version.

flags.excerpt: you grant TikTok an unconditional, irrevocable, non-exclusive, royalty-free, fully transferable worldwide licence
flags.explanation: TikTok can use any content you post, anywhere in the world, forever. This broad license may prevent full deletion of your content.

flags.excerpt: any disputes will be resolved by binding arbitration on an individual basis
flags.explanation: You give up your right to sue TikTok in court or join a class action. Disputes go to arbitration instead — on their terms.

flags.excerpt: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time
flags.explanation: TikTok can change the rules whenever they want. Continuing to use the app is treated as acceptance.

note: Pre-built analysis — TikTok Terms of Service (EEA, 2024). Does not cover the Privacy Policy — click Re-analyse on the Privacy Policy page for a fresh analysis.

flags.excerpt: you grant us a non-exclusive, royalty-free, transferable, sub-licensable, worldwide license
flags.explanation: Instagram (Meta) can use, share and sublicense your photos and videos. This broad license may outlast your account deletion.

flags.excerpt: We can remove any content or information you share on our Services if we believe it violates these Terms
flags.explanation: Instagram can delete your content or account for violating broadly-defined community standards, with limited appeal options.

note: Pre-built analysis — Instagram shares T&Cs with Meta (2024).

flags.excerpt: you grant YouTube a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free, sublicensable and transferable license
flags.explanation: YouTube can use your videos across all Google services. The license survives even if you delete your content.

flags.excerpt: YouTube reserves the right to discontinue any aspect of the YouTube Service at any time
flags.explanation: Google can shut down features or your channel with notice, but the decision is entirely theirs.

flags.excerpt: YouTube is not responsible for the accuracy, usefulness, safety, or intellectual property rights
flags.explanation: YouTube limits its liability for third-party content and technical failures. Your compensation rights may be restricted.

note: Pre-built analysis — YouTube Terms of Service (2024).

flags.excerpt: YouTube may collect information about you from third parties and combine that with information we collect about you
flags.explanation: Google combines your YouTube activity with data from other Google services and third parties to build an extensive profile, limiting your practical right to erasure.

flags.excerpt: your information may be processed in the United States or any other country in which Google or its agents maintain facilities
flags.explanation: Your data is transferred outside the EEA to the US and other countries. GDPR Art. 46 safeguards apply, but enforcement against US authorities remains limited.

flags.excerpt: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of any changes by posting the new Privacy Policy on this page
flags.explanation: Google can update its privacy terms unilaterally. A banner notification does not require your active consent to the new terms.

flags.excerpt: we retain certain information you share with us even after you delete your account
flags.explanation: Some data persists after account deletion — this may conflict with your GDPR right to erasure (Art. 17).

note: Pre-built analysis — YouTube/Google Privacy Policy (2024). Re-analyse for the latest version.

flags.excerpt: you grant us a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free license with the right to sublicense
flags.explanation: X/Twitter can use your tweets, images, and videos anywhere — including for training AI — though you retain ownership.

flags.excerpt: We may revise these Terms from time to time
flags.explanation: X can update its terms at any time. You'll be notified, but continued use means acceptance of the new terms.

flags.excerpt: Any disputes will be resolved by arbitration in San Francisco, California
flags.explanation: Disputes must go to arbitration in California, far from most users. Class action lawsuits are not permitted.

note: Pre-built analysis — X (Twitter) Terms of Service (2024).

flags.excerpt: We may make changes to these Terms from time to time
flags.explanation: Spotify can update terms with notice. Continued use after the effective date means acceptance.

flags.excerpt: to the extent permitted under applicable law, Spotify shall not be liable
flags.explanation: Spotify limits its liability for service disruptions and data issues to the maximum extent the law allows.

note: Pre-built analysis — Spotify Terms of Service (2024). Relatively balanced compared to social platforms.
